items.title: Feature creation in the ML pipeline
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  raw["raw columns"] --> clean["clean / type cast"]
  clean --> create["feature creation: ratios, datetime, flags"]
  create --> encode["encode / scale / extract"]
  encode --> model["train estimator"]
items.caption: Creation happens after basic cleaning and before learned encoders or scaling in most tabular workflows.

items.title: Derived vs interaction features
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  a["price"] --> ratio["price / sqft → derived"]
  b["sqft"] --> ratio
  c["promo_depth"] --> inter["promo × is_holiday → interaction"]
  d["is_holiday"] --> inter
items.caption: Derived features combine columns into one new signal; interactions capture when one column modulates another's effect.

items.title: Datetime decomposition
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  ts["timestamp column"] --> parse["pd.to_datetime"]
  parse --> parts["hour, dayofweek, month"]
  parse --> elapsed["days_since_event"]
  parts --> cyclic["sin/cos for periodic fields"]
items.caption: Parse once, then branch into calendar parts, elapsed durations, and optional cyclical encodings.

items.title: Groupby aggregate join pattern
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  orders["orders table"] --> grp["groupby customer_id"]
  grp --> agg["agg count, sum, mean"]
  cust["customers table"] --> join["merge on customer_id"]
  agg --> join
  join --> row["one row per customer with history features"]
items.caption: Aggregates computed at event grain are joined back to the modeling grain as derived history columns.
